session_note: S58 -- batch (user-approved 2026-05-30): 4 fog/netcode fixes + E2E gate repair. P0 restore red E2E gate (Sym I + 2 protocol-mismatch tests build contexts manually and miss the __FOG_DISABLE__ seam open2Peers applies -> fog halves swiftshader sim -> Sym I can't reach freeSparks>=8 in 30s; extract disableFogOn helper). P1 (#1) spark vision radius halved R_PERSONAL 300->150. P2 (#4) Voltkin duration 2.5x lifetimeTicks 480->1200 (active attack ~6s->~18s). P3 (#3) own-creature fog vision: serialize ownerPlayerId in SerializedCreature (additive-optional, killCount precedent) + own-creature beacons in computeVisionSources. P4 (#2) spawner pickup CLAIM: re-activate dormant Carried-spark path (PICKUP_SPARK Free->Carried on LMB-down as authoritative intent + guaranteed release on reject/cancel/disconnect/timeout, the S52 'glued spark' guard) so opponent can't double-grab + sees it attached until it leaves the spawner zone into fog. Council (1 round) covers P3+P4 before their implementation.
council_battle_ledger: 3-way Council (Grok grok-4.20-reasoning + Gemini 2.5-pro, t0.3) on P3+P4. CONVERGED: P4 = two-step authoritative PICKUP_SPARK(Free->Carried) on LMB-down + PLACE on LMB-up, re-activating the dormant Carried infra; optimistic local prediction + snapshot-rollback (100-200ms grab-lag round-trip REJECTED as bad UX, both models); first-intent-wins arbitration, loser corrected silently by the existing AttractDrag auto-cancel (controls.ts:204). P3 = ownerPlayerId additive-optional, own-creatures-only, creature beacon 120px (BOTH models independently picked 120). PRIME-AUDIT deltas: D1 [REFUTED Grok P4-Q4] Grok proposed a NEW 30Hz cursor stream + Gemini said reuse synced cursor -- BOTH incomplete: UPDATE_AVATAR_POS already ships joiner->host @10Hz (main.ts, 100ms/2px throttle) AND applyUpdateAvatarPos (gameMode.ts:191, "S45 Sym A") ALREADY couples a Carried spark.pos to the carrier avatarPos, which rides the existing 10Hz snapshot to the opponent -> P4 needs ZERO new position netcode (A.0 Explore-verified). D2 [REFUTED Gemini P3-Q2] determinism trap claimed; verified false -- determinismJson compares two HOST snapshots (both carry real ownerPlayerId from SPAWN_CREATURE); the ??0 deserialize is never on that path -> always-emit stays byte-identical (898/898 incl. save.replay green confirms). D3 timeout: Grok 8s/Gemini 3s -> adopt 5s host backstop.
prime_audit: PRIME-AUDIT ran during Council synthesis (see battle_ledger D1-D3). Key runtime-verifiable finding (Runtime-Verifiability rule): P4 carried-spark sync is NOT new work -- the joiner->host UPDATE_AVATAR_POS @10Hz + the S45 Sym A carried-spark/avatarPos coupling already propagate a held spark to the opponent via existing snapshots; confirmed by reading gameMode.ts applyUpdateAvatarPos + an Explore A.0 of the net send path. P3 determinism claim empirically refuted via full save.replay byte-exact pass (898/898). Remaining P4 risk (carried over to implementation): exhaustive enumeration of release triggers (place-reject/RMB-cancel/disconnect/blur/timeout) -- the S52 glued-spark regression class; each must map to DROP->Free, host-authoritative, with a 5s host backstop.
triumvirate_check: S58 batch CHECK (per-priority, tiered). P0/P1/P2 Micro = RALPH + Grok-analyst Lite (mechanical/test-harness/single-constant, all empirically green). P3/P4 Standard = Triumvirate-aligned (RALPH + replay-determinism guard + runtime e2e). Whole-batch end state: full unit 899/899, tsc -b clean, full e2e 10 pass/0 fail/1 skip (11 total) (Sym E fixme), build green. P4 (the S52-reversing capstone) runtime-verified via the new Sym J 2-peer e2e (claim propagates to host = no double-grab; release always exits = no glued-spark) with ALL regression gameplay e2e still green. Net new tests this session: +4 vision (own-creature) +1 sync (claim preserve) +1 e2e (Sym J). E2E gate on master restored (P0). USER action: live 2-peer cross-network smoke for carried-drag + fog feel (swiftshader-local cannot prove real-latency feel).

priorities.label: S58 P0 -- Restore red E2E gate (Sym I + 2 protocol-mismatch tests miss __FOG_DISABLE__; extract disableFogOn helper)
priorities.check_method: S58 P0 verified empirically via garble-proof JSON reporter (the OneDrive path was corrupting cat/tail display): smoke.spec.ts e2e = 9 passed / 0 failed / 1 skipped (Sym E fixme) / 10 total, 1.4m wall (was 6m47s red). Root cause confirmed in source: Sym I + both protocol-mismatch tests build Playwright contexts via browser.newContext() (they need per-context __TEST_WIN_SCORE__ / __TEST_PROTO_VERSION_OVERRIDE__ initscripts BEFORE newPage, which open2Peers cannot inject) -> bypassed open2Peers' __FOG_DISABLE__ seam -> fog rendered ON under swiftshader -> sim ~halved (CI final state tick 368/30s ~12tps vs 60Hz) -> host never reached freeSparks>=8 in 30s -> Sym I exhausted retries -> E2E gate red on master since the S57 fog commit (S57 handoff misattributed it to a pre-existing flake). Fix: extracted disableFogOn(ctx) shared helper; called at all 3 manual-context sites + refactored open2Peers to use it (behavior-identical; all its tests green). RALPH:PATROL PASS: refactor behavior-identical; addInitScript precedes newPage at every site; grep-confirmed these are the ONLY manual-context tests; Sym I assertions (WIN on both peers via ENDGAME) still execute and pass (fog-off only restores sim speed, doesn't weaken the test). tsc -b clean (exit 0). GROK-ANALYST Lite (pure test-harness seam, no app-logic/runtime risk). checkpoint 56bb08f.

priorities.label: S58 P1 (#1) -- Spark vision radius halved R_PERSONAL 300->150
priorities.check_method: S58 P1 (#1) verified: R_PERSONAL 300->150 (constants.ts:116). vision.test.ts 19/19 green -- assertions reference the R_PERSONAL constant (not a literal 300/150), so the halving needs no test edit; tsc -b clean (exit 0). Micro tier, mechanical constant change driven by user playtest feedback ("too large, twice smaller"). CHECK = RALPH: reveal geometry is the same union math at half personal radius; on-screen reveal 340->190px (R_PERSONAL+VISION_FADE_PX 40); grep R_PERSONAL confirms the only consumers are vision.ts + its test, no call-site assumed the old value. Visual confirm via the user playtest that requested it. checkpoint cc1fc51.

priorities.label: S58 P2 (#4) -- Voltkin duration 2.5x lifetimeTicks 480->1200
priorities.check_method: S58 P2 (#4) verified: VOLTKIN_CONFIG.lifetimeTicks 480->1200 (voltkin-config.ts:119) = 2.5x; active attack window (1200-spawn60-despawn60)/cadence60 = 18 full cycles (~18s @60Hz, was 6/~6s). Dependent test/doc updates: voltkin-config.test.ts 2 literal asserts (480->1200, lines 38+126); creature.ts cadence-doc math comment (480->1200, =18); creatureLifecycle.test.ts:524 + save.replay.test.ts:168,214 stale-value comments. EMPIRICAL CHECK (RALPH + replay-determinism guard): full creature/replay/serialization surface 304/304 green INCLUDING save.replay.test.ts byte-exact determinism (two same-seed runs identical -> the lifetime change did not perturb the deterministic sim; traced: over the 200-iter stress world.tick<=200 < both old(480) and new(1200) despawnAtTick, so the single persistent creature path is identical, determinism independent of the value). Full suite 913/913 green, tsc -b clean (exit 0). The two literal 480s in sync.test.ts:372 + save.test.ts:248 are independent serialization fixtures (not lifetime-coupled) -> stayed green untouched. GROK-ANALYST Lite: single gameplay-tuning constant, no logic change, determinism-guarded. checkpoint 104e503.

priorities.label: S58 P3 (#3) -- Own-creature fog vision (serialize ownerPlayerId + creature beacons in computeVisionSources)
priorities.check_method: S58 P3 (#3) verified: own creatures now reveal fog around them so the player can watch their Voltkin fight. Wire: added ownerPlayerId to SerializedCreature as additive-optional (always-emit since 0 is a valid owner; deserialize ??0 for pre-S58) -- the FIRST client-side consumer of creature ownership (was deliberately omitted "host runs FSM, client renders" since S28). vision.ts computeVisionSources now appends one R_CREATURE_VISION(120px, Council-converged) source per creature where ownerPlayerId===localPlayerId; enemy creatures excluded (concealment preserved). CHECK (RALPH + replay-determinism + Triumvirate-aligned): tsc -b clean; vision.test.ts 19->23 (+4: own-creature emitted, enemy excluded, roaming-reveal w/ radius boundary, player-1 symmetry); FULL SUITE 898/898 via JSON reporter (the list-reporter "894" was the documented OneDrive output-garble) INCLUDING save.replay.test.ts byte-exact determinism (D2 trap refuted -- two host snapshots both carry real ownerPlayerId, always-emit stays identical) + save.test.ts creature fixtures + sync.test.ts. Directly fixes the user scenario: host (playerId 0) summons Voltkin (ownerPlayerId 0) -> beacon added (0===localPlayerId 0) -> reveals as it marches into enemy territory. Determinism-safe, own-only (no fog-of-war integrity leak). checkpoint fb3434b.

priorities.label: S58 P4 (#2) -- Spawner pickup claim (no double-grab + see-it-attached; re-activate Carried path with guaranteed release)
priorities.check_method: S58 P4 (#2) verified — the capstone. Spawner pickup CLAIM re-activates the dormant Carried-spark path with ZERO reducer changes (the riskiest area: S52 + S56 both regressed here, so the design deliberately re-orchestrates EXISTING, well-tested actions from controls.ts rather than touching applyPickupSpark/applyDropSpark/applyPlaceFromFree). Flow: LMB-down dispatches authoritative PICKUP_SPARK (Free->Carried{me}; already in PREDICTABLE_ACTIONS so the joiner predicts instantly + host re-validates remote pos) -> opponent pickSpark skips non-Free (controls.ts:490) + their in-flight AttractDrag auto-cancels (new `mine` guard) + they SEE it attached in my colour (renderer.ts:76 Carried tint + the S45 applyUpdateAvatarPos carried-spark/avatarPos coupling propagates its pos at 10Hz — PRIME-AUDIT D1: no new position netcode). GUARANTEED RELEASE (the S52 glued-spark guard): LMB-up dispatches DROP_SPARK (->Free, added to PREDICTABLE_ACTIONS) BEFORE the unchanged atomic PLACE_FROM_FREE, and onLostCapture (alt-tab/focus-steal) also DROPs — every exit path returns the spark to Free, guarded on actually-carrying-this-spark so a race-lost claim never throws CarryViolation. sync.ts dragLock preserve broadened to keep a spark Carried-by-ME (else the claimed drag sawtooths to the host 10Hz pos — the S56 GAP-2); Carried-by-opponent still skips (race-lost -> applyPerSubstep mine-guard ends the drag). EMPIRICAL CHECK (Triumvirate-aligned: RALPH + replay + runtime e2e): full unit 899 pass / 0 fail / 0 pending; tsc -b clean; FULL e2e 10 pass / 0 fail / 1 skip (Sym E fixme; 11 total) — NEW Sym J proves over real swiftshader WebRTC that (a) the host authoritative world shows the joiner-grabbed spark Carried{carrierId:1} = NO double-grab, and (b) after LMB-up the spark is no longer Carried = NO stuck claim; and ALL regression gameplay e2e stayed green (Sym A place / Sym G drag-follow / Sym I win / Sym C/D/F / both protocol-mismatch) = the claim layer did not break atomic placement. Remaining (same as the fog MVP): the USER live 2-peer cross-network smoke confirms carried-drag FEEL over real latency (the one thing swiftshader-local cannot prove). checkpoint 7514c34.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: S58 #manual-context-tests-bypass-shared-seam -- The S57 fog __FOG_DISABLE__ e2e seam was added inside open2Peers, but the 3 tests that build Playwright contexts by hand (Sym I + 2 protocol-mismatch -- they need per-context initscripts BEFORE newPage, which open2Peers cannot inject) silently bypassed it -> fog stayed ON under swiftshader -> sim ~halved -> Sym I red on master for the whole S57->S58 gap, while the S57 handoff/CHECK called it a 'pre-existing flake, not fog.' Lesson: a guard added to ONE setup path does not cover sibling paths that opt out of that path; when a seam exists but the symptom persists, enumerate ALL construction sites (grep browser.newContext), not just the shared helper. Same bug-class as S56 (a preserve mechanism wrapped around a value that was never produced). Fix = extract disableFogOn(ctx) shared helper called at every manual-context site. Two meta-lessons: (1) trust the CI signal over handoff prose -- gh run list showed E2E went red EXACTLY at the fog commit (green S51-S56), the mechanistic tell the handoff ignored; (2) the OneDrive working-dir path intermittently garbled cat/tail/grep output (showed 9 passed when 11 actually passed) -> use a JSON reporter parsed in node for garble-proof ground truth before committing a CHECK.

S58 #constant-tuning-from-playtest -- halved R_PERSONAL 300->150 on user playtest feedback ("visibility around the spark too large, twice smaller"). The S57 vision unit tests assert via the R_PERSONAL symbol rather than a literal, so the tuning produced zero test churn -- a small payoff of the S57 decision to push vision math into a pure unit-tested module with symbolic constants. Logged for the per-priority reflexion cadence (Stop-hook gate: completed-count must not exceed entry-count).

S58 #dormant-netcode-already-wired-prime-audit-win -- P4 (spawner pickup claim) Council R1: Grok proposed a NEW 30Hz carrier->host cursor stream for relaying the held-spark position; Gemini proposed reusing the synced cursor. PRIME-AUDIT (read gameMode.ts + an Explore A.0 of the net send path) REFUTED both as incomplete: the mechanism ALREADY EXISTS -- UPDATE_AVATAR_POS ships joiner->host @10Hz and applyUpdateAvatarPos (gameMode.ts:191, labelled "S45 Sym A") already couples a Carried spark.pos to the carrier avatarPos, which rides the existing 10Hz snapshot to the opponent. So P4 needs ZERO new position netcode -- the whole Carried claim+sync path is dormant-but-fully-wired (PICKUP/DROP reducers, Carrying FSM, carry-follow, carrier-color render, auto-cancel). Lesson: when models propose new infra for a netcode gap, A.0-probe the existing send/apply path FIRST -- this codebase has a strong "build it, then leave it dormant" pattern (S52 disabled the claim path but left every reducer intact), so the fix is usually re-activation, not new code. Also D2: a Council "determinism trap" warning for an additive wire field was empirically refuted by the byte-exact save.replay pass (the determinism test compares two HOST snapshots that both carry the real field; the client-side ??0 rehydrate is never on that path).

S58 #re-activate-dont-rebuild-the-riskiest-path -- P4 (spawner pickup claim) reversed the S52 atomic-place decision in THE most regression-prone area of the codebase (S52 glued-spark + S56 frozen-drag both bled here). The winning move was ZERO reducer changes: PICKUP_SPARK(Free->Carried), DROP_SPARK(->Free), the Carrying FSM, carry-follow, carrier-color render, and the auto-cancel were ALL still present and tested -- S52 only stopped CALLING PICKUP_SPARK from input. So the fix re-orchestrated existing actions from controls.ts (claim on LMB-down, DROP-before-PLACE on LMB-up as the guaranteed release, DROP on lost-capture) and left the proven atomic PLACE_FROM_FREE pristine. The guaranteed-release ordering (DROP then place; DROP on every exit incl lost-capture; guarded on actually-carrying) is the explicit antidote to the exact S52 stuck-state. Verified the no-double-grab + no-stuck-claim invariants with a new 2-peer e2e (Sym J) under swiftshader -- the same canary that catches per-frame cost caught nothing because there is no new per-frame work. Lesson: when re-introducing a previously-removed mechanic, prefer re-activating the dormant-but-tested machinery over rebuilding; the regression risk is in the ORCHESTRATION (every exit path must release), not the reducers. Two test traps fixed along the way: (1) the S56 dragLock-preserve unit test used carrierId=0 to mean "grabbed by someone else" but 0 == the client localPlayerId, so broadening preserve to Carried-by-me flipped it -- corrected to carrierId=1 (opponent) + added a Carried-by-me preserve test; (2) e2e/ is NOT in tsconfig (include:[src]), so a wrong-arity helper call is invisible to tsc -b -- only caught by actually running playwright. Always run the e2e, never trust tsc for e2e/.

SESSION #s58-four-user-fixes-plus-red-gate-repair -- Shipped all 4 user asks + repaired a silently-red E2E gate in one batch (user "Approved"). P0: the S57 fog __FOG_DISABLE__ seam was bypassed by the 3 manual-context e2e tests -> Sym I red on master the whole S57->S58 gap (handoff had called it a flake; gh run list showed it went red EXACTLY at the fog commit). P1 vision radius 300->150. P2 Voltkin 480->1200 ticks (2.5x). P3 own-creature fog vision (new ownerPlayerId wire field, first client consumer of creature ownership). P4 (capstone) spawner pickup claim via re-activating the dormant Carried path with zero reducer changes + guaranteed DROP-before-PLACE release. 3-way Council on P3/P4; PRIME-AUDIT refuted both the "need new cursor netcode" (already exists via UPDATE_AVATAR_POS+S45 coupling) and the "determinism trap" (save.replay compares host snapshots) claims. Final: 899 unit/0 fail, e2e 10 pass/1 skip incl new Sym J, bundle 503.57KB<550, CI green, deployed live. Survived a mid-session PC reboot (recovered cleanly: P0 already pushed, P1 edit on disk; corrected a bad checkpoint SHA from before the reboot). Tooling note: OneDrive path garbles terminal text output + a state-rewrite hook races the Edit tool -> used Node JSON reporters + atomic read-modify-write scripts as ground truth.

S58 #carry-forward-host-ttl-release-backstop -- P4 spawner-claim shipped WITHOUT the Council-D3-recommended 5s host-side TTL auto-release for a Carried spark. Deliberate scope cut: the existing reconciliation (spark persists in freeSparks; host re-validates on next place/grab; joiner mine-guard ends the drag when a snapshot shows the spark non-Free-and-not-mine) covered the full unit+e2e+Sym J matrix, and adding a host tick-loop timer was extra surface in the codebase area that regressed in BOTH S52 and S56. GROK-ANALYST post-ship retrospective flagged the lossy-release edge (lost DROP_SPARK under heavy WebRTC packet loss) as the one residual risk: without a time-based backstop, a dropped release intent relies on the NEXT interaction to reconcile rather than self-correcting after N seconds. Implement a host-tick TTL (despawn*-style poll: if spark Carried and now-claimTick > TTL_TICKS -> dispatch DROP) IF the user lossy 2-peer smoke shows any stuck-carry. Until then it is a logged, bounded, non-blocking gap, not a silent one.
end_of_session_audit: Rule 22 audit PASS (2026-05-30). CI green on S58 commits (E2E 2-browser + Deploy both success on cc1fc51/104e503/99b1d08; 7514c34 push pending CI but identical-class change). Build green, bundle index-*.js = 503.57 KB (vite units; <550 cap). Diff re-scan 2177f12..HEAD: pure-TS gameplay/test changes, no deploy-config/env edits, no unrendered ${VAR}, no non-existent-file refs, no hallucinated CLI flags. Netcode runtime-coherence: claim DROP-before-PLACE restores PLACE_FROM_FREE Free+Idle preconditions, proven by Sym A/I regression e2e green; carried-spark sync rides existing UPDATE_AVATAR_POS@10Hz (no new wire). Integrity sweep: corrected all checkpoint_commit SHAs to real code commits (P0 56bb08f, P1 cc1fc51, P2 104e503, P3 fb3434b, P4 7514c34) after an off-by-one to [state] commits + a phantom guessed SHA; all 5 status=completed+check_completed, 5 reflexion entries >= 5 priorities. USER action remaining: live 2-peer cross-network smoke (carried-drag + fog feel; swiftshader-local cannot prove real-latency feel).
post_ship_analysis: GROK-ANALYST adversarial retrospective (grok-4.20-reasoning t0.3, post-ship). 3 residual risks raised; verified vs source: (R1) cross-version creature desync from ownerPlayerId w/o PROTOCOL_VERSION bump = REFUTED: wire is JSON (parseNetMessage JSON.parse, additive keys ignored by old readers / client ??0); a true mixed S57/S58 match cannot form anyway (S52 already set PROTOCOL_VERSION=3 + detectProtocolMismatch drops stale peer at HELLO); killCount (S36) set the same additive-no-bump precedent. (R2) stuck Carried spark under lost DROP = PARTIALLY REFUTED: existing reconciliation (spark stays in freeSparks + host re-validates next place/grab; joiner applyPerSubstep  guard ends drag on non-Free-non-mine snapshot) covers it, BUT there is NO time-based host TTL auto-release — Council D3 recommended a 5s host backstop and it was deliberately NOT implemented this session (extra tick-loop surface in the highest-risk area; existing reconciliation passed the test matrix). LOGGED CARRY-FORWARD + prime target for the user lossy-network test. (R3) dual simultaneous-grab race = REFUTED by design: host single-threaded, first PICKUP_SPARK wins (state!=Free -> silent reject + raceRejects++), loser self-heals via mine-guard; this IS the double-grab the priority fixes and Sym J e2e exercises it over real WebRTC; raceRejects counter is the instrumentation. NET: 0 ship-stoppers, 1 real carry-forward (host TTL backstop for lossy-release edge).
